items.name: trail
items.description: Trail renderer for fading ribbon effects.

items.name: viewport
items.description: Virtual resolution viewport with manual transform application.

items.name: viewport_scale
items.description: Virtual resolution viewport with automatic scaling.

graphics::nine_slice.module_doc: Nine-slice (9-patch) image rendering for scalable UI elements.  A nine-slice divides a texture into 9 regions using 4 border insets: corners are drawn at fixed size, edges stretch in one axis, and the center stretches in both axes. This preserves visual quality when scaling UI panels, buttons, and dialog boxes.

items.name: Patch
items.description: A single patch rectangle: `(src_x, src_y, src_w, src_h, dst_x, dst_y, dst_w, dst_h)`.

items.name: NineSlice
items.description: A nine-slice image definition: a texture plus border insets.  The four insets (`top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`) define pixel distances from each edge of the source texture. These divide the texture into 9 regions:  ```text ┌───────┬─────────────────┬───────┐ │ TL    │    Top Edge      │  TR   │ ├───────┼─────────────────┼───────┤ │ Left  │    Center        │ Right │ │ Edge  │   (stretches)    │ Edge  │ ├───────┼─────────────────┼───────┤ │ BL    │   Bottom Edge    │  BR   │ └───────┴─────────────────┴───────┘ ```  Corners maintain their original pixel dimensions. Edges stretch along one axis. The center stretches in both axes.  # Fields - `e` — `:textures` for the source image.`. - `texture_key` — `TextureKey`. - `top` — `f32`. - `right` — `f32`. - `bottom` — `f32`. - `left` — `f32`. - `tex_width` — `f32`. - `tex_height` — `f32`.

graphics::palette_lut.module_doc: Color palette lookup table for shader-based palette swapping.  Maps source colors to target colors, allowing sprites to be re-colored at render time without modifying the original texture.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for palette lut-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `PaletteLUT`. Primary functions: `new()`, `get_color_count()`, `set_color()`, `get_from_color()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: PaletteLUT
items.description: Color palette lookup table mapping source colors to target colors.  # Fields - `from_colors` — `Vec<Color>`. - `to_colors` — `Vec<Color>`.  Each entry pairs a "from" color with a "to" color. At render time a shader can replace pixels matching a source color with the corresponding target color.

graphics::polygon_map.module_doc: Polygon map renderer with region management and hit detection.  Manages a collection of named polygon regions with colors, labels, highlighting, and point-in-polygon queries.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for polygon map-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `PolygonRegion`, `PolygonMap`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add_region()`, `remove_region()`, `set_region_color()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: PolygonRegion
items.description: A named polygon region. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `vertices` — `Vec<f32>`. - `color` — `Color`. - `label` — `Option<String>`. - `font_size` — `f32`.

items.name: PolygonMap
items.description: Polygon map renderer with region management and hit detection.  # Fields - `outline_color` — `Color`. - `outline_width` — `f32`. - `highlight_color` — `Color`. - `highlighted` — `Option<String>`.  Stores named polygon regions and supports point-in-polygon queries, highlighting, and bounding-box computation. Regions are keyed by name.

graphics::postfx.module_doc: Post-processing effects module.  Provides a stackable post-processing pipeline with built-in effects (bloom, blur, CRT, vignette, chromatic aberration, colour grading, godrays) and custom shader passes. Effects are organized into a `PostFxStack` that captures the scene via ping-pong canvases, applies all enabled effects in order, and outputs the final result.

items.name: PostFxEffectType
items.description: Built-in effect types for the post-processing pipeline.  Each variant produces a different full-screen shader pass.  # Variants - `R` — R variant. - `Bloom` — Bloom variant. - `Gaussian` — Gaussian variant. - `Blur` — Blur variant. - `T` — T variant. - `Crt` — Crt variant. - `Light` — Light variant. - `Godrays` — Godrays variant. - `Screen` — Screen variant. - `Vignette` — Vignette variant. - `Colour` — Colour variant. - `ColourGrade` — ColourGrade variant. - `Chromatic` — Chromatic variant. - `Pass` — Pass variant. - `Custom` — Custom variant.

items.name: PostFxEffect
items.description: A single post-processing effect with named float parameters.  # Fields - `effect_type` — `PostFxEffectType` — The kind of effect. - `params` — Named float parameters controlling the effect. - `enabled` — Whether this effect is active in its stack. - `shader_id` — Optional custom shader handle (for `Custom` type).

items.name: PostFxStack
items.description: An ordered chain of effects that captures and processes the rendered scene.  # Fields - `effects` — Ordered list of effect indices. - `enabled` — Per-effect enable state (parallel to `effects`). - `width` — Canvas width in pixels. - `height` — Canvas height in pixels.  The stack manages ping-pong canvases internally for multi-pass rendering. During `luna.draw`, the user calls `beginCapture()` → draws scene → `endCapture()` → `apply()` to render the post-processed result.

graphics::renderer.module_doc: Draw command types, blend modes, and texture data for the Luna2D rendering pipeline.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for renderer-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `CompareMode`, `StencilAction`, `TextAlign`, `DrawMode`, `BlendMode`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: CompareMode
items.description: Stencil comparison mode for `luna.graphics.setStencilTest`.  # Variants - `Equal` — Equal variant. - `NotEqual` — NotEqual variant. - `Less` — Less variant. - `LessEqual` — LessEqual variant. - `Greater` — Greater variant. - `GreaterEqual` — GreaterEqual variant. - `Always` — Always variant. - `Never` — Never variant.

items.name: StencilAction
items.description: Stencil write action for `luna.graphics.stencil`.  # Variants - `Replace` — Replace variant. - `Increment` — Increment variant. - `Decrement` — Decrement variant. - `IncrementWrap` — IncrementWrap variant. - `DecrementWrap` — DecrementWrap variant.

items.name: TextAlign
items.description: Text alignment mode for formatted text printing.  # Variants - `Left` — Left variant. - `Center` — Center variant. - `Right` — Right variant. - `Justify` — Justify variant.

items.name: DrawMode
items.description: Whether a shape is drawn filled or as an outline.  # Variants - `Fill` — Draw shapes as solid filled areas. - `Line` — Draw shapes as outlines using the current line width.

items.name: BlendMode
items.description: Blending mode for draw operations. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Alpha` — Alpha variant. - `Add` — Add variant. - `Multiply` — Multiply variant. - `Replace` — Replace variant. - `Screen` — Screen variant.

items.name: DrawCommand
items.description: A single deferred draw operation queued during `luna.draw()` and executed by `GpuRenderer`.  # Variants - `SetColor(r, g, b, a)` — Sets the active draw color; affects all subsequent draw calls. - `Rectangle` — Draws an axis-aligned rectangle. - `RoundedRectangle` — Draws a rectangle with rounded corners. - `Circle` — Draws a circle at center `(x, y)` with radius `r`. - `Ellipse` — Draws an ellipse with semi-axes `rx`, `ry`. - `Triangle` — Draws a triangle with three explicit vertices. - `Polygon` — Draws an arbitrary polygon from a flat `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]` vertex list. - `Line` — Draws a line segment from `(x1, y1)` to `(x2, y2)`. - `Polyline` — Draws a connected sequence of line segments through a flat `[x0,y0,x1,y1,…]` list. - `DrawImage` — Blits a texture (by index) at position `(x, y)`. - `DrawImageEx` — Draws a texture with full transform (rotation, scale, origin offset). - `DrawQuad` — Draws a sub-region (quad) of a texture with full transform. - `Print` — Renders text using the built-in bitmap font. - `SetLineWidth` — Sets the stroke width for subsequent outline draws. - `PushTransform` — Saves the current transform onto the stack. - `PopTransform` — Restores the previous transform from the stack. - `Translate` — Applies a translation to the current transform. - `Rotate` — Applies a rotation (in radians) to the current transform. - `Scale` — Applies a scale to the current transform. - `Arc` — Draws an arc (sector or outline) of a circle.

items.name: TextureData
items.description: Raw RGBA pixel data for a loaded texture, stored in the renderer's texture atlas.  # Fields - `pixels` — Raw RGBA byte data in RGBA format. - `width` — Texture width in pixels. - `height` — Texture height in pixels.

graphics::shader.module_doc: Custom WGSL shader support for Luna2D.  Allows Lua scripts to create and apply custom fragment shaders with uniform variables.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for shader-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ShaderFragmentInput`, `Shader`, `UniformValue`. Primary functions: `new()`, `send()`, `has_uniform()`, `ordered_uniforms()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ShaderFragmentInput
items.description: Which fragment shader input the user's entry point expects.  # Variants - `Color` — Color variant. - `Uv` — Uv variant.

items.name: Shader
items.description: Represents a compiled custom shader with its uniform values.  # Fields - `source` — `String`. - `wrapper_source` — `String`. - `fragment_entry_name` — `String`. - `fragment_inputs` — `Vec<ShaderFragmentInput>`. - `uniforms` — `HashMap<String, UniformValue>`.  Currently stores shader source and uniforms. The GPU pipeline is created lazily when the shader is first used during rendering.

items.name: UniformValue
items.description: A uniform value that can be sent to a shader from Lua.  # Variants - `Float` — Float variant. - `Vec2` — Vec2 variant. - `Vec3` — Vec3 variant. - `Vec4` — Vec4 variant. - `Int` — Int variant. - `Bool` — Bool variant.

graphics::sprite.module_doc: Sprite implementation for the `graphics` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for sprite-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Sprite`. Primary functions: `new()`, `set_position()`, `set_scale()`, `set_rotation()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Sprite
items.description: A textured game object with position, scale, rotation, and tint color.  `Sprite` acts as a transform + tint wrapper around a `Texture`. It does not own the texture; it references it by id in the renderer's texture atlas.  # Fields - `texture_id` — Index into the renderer's texture atlas. - `position` — World-space position of the sprite's origin. - `scale` — Per-axis scale factor; `Vec2::ONE` = original size. - `rotation` — Rotation in radians. - `color` — Multiplicative tint applied to the texture; `Color::WHITE` = no tint.

graphics::sprite_batch.module_doc: Sprite batching for efficient rendering of many sprites sharing one texture.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for sprite batch-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SpriteBatch`, `BatchEntry`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add()`, `clear()`, `texture_key()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SpriteBatch
items.description: A batch of sprites sharing a single texture, drawn in one GPU call.  Created via `luna.graphics.newSpriteBatch(image_id, max_sprites?)`. Use `add()` to queue sprites and the engine draws them all at once.  # Fields - `e` — `:textures`.`. - `texture_key` — `TextureKey`. - `entries` — `Vec<BatchEntry>`. - `max_entries` — `usize`.

items.name: BatchEntry
items.description: A single sprite in a batch, describing position, region, and transform.  # Fields - `x` — `f32`. - `y` — `f32`. - `quad_x` — `f32`. - `quad_y` — `f32`. - `quad_w` — `f32`. - `quad_h` — `f32`. - `rotation` — `f32`. - `sx` — `f32`. - `sy` — `f32`. - `ox` — `f32`. - `oy` — `f32`.

graphics::sprite_sheet.module_doc: Grid-based sprite sheet with directional support and named groups.  Splits a texture into a uniform grid of frames and provides indexed, row/column, named-group, and directional access to the resulting quads.

items.name: FrameGroup
items.description: Named frame group within the sprite sheet.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `start_frame` — `usize`. - `count` — `usize`.

items.name: DirectionLayout
items.description: Directional layout for sprite sets. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Rows` — Rows variant. - `Columns` — Columns variant.

items.name: SpriteSheet
items.description: Grid-based sprite sheet with directional support and named groups.  # Fields - `frame_width` — `u32`. - `frame_height` — `u32`. - `columns` — `u32`. - `rows` — `u32`. - `texture_width` — `u32`. - `texture_height` — `u32`.  Divides a texture into equal-sized cells and pre-computes UV quads for every frame. Supports named groups and 4/8-directional layouts.

graphics::srgb.module_doc: Srgb implementation for the `graphics` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for srgb-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Color`. Primary functions: `from_u8()`, `to_u8()`, `to_rgb_u32()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Color
items.description: RGBA color stored as `f32` components in the range `[0.0, 1.0]`.  Used everywhere the API accepts a color: `luna.graphics.setColor`, sprite tints, background color, etc.  # Fields - `r` — Red channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `g` — Green channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `b` — Blue channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `a` — Alpha channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`; `1.0` = fully opaque.

graphics::texture.module_doc: Texture implementation for the `graphics` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for texture-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Texture`. Primary functions: `load()`, `from_rgba()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Texture
items.description: A loaded image asset referenced by its index into the renderer's texture list.  `Texture` is a lightweight handle; the actual pixel data lives in `TextureData` inside `Renderer::textures` (or `SharedState::textures`).  # Fields - `id` — Index into the renderer's `TextureData` list. - `width` — Image width in pixels. - `height` — Image height in pixels.

graphics::texture_atlas.module_doc: CPU-side bin-packing texture atlas using a shelf algorithm.  Packs named rectangular regions into a fixed-size atlas without any GPU interaction. Useful for building sprite-sheet layouts at load time.

items.name: AtlasRegion
items.description: A named rectangular region packed into the atlas.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `x` — `u32`. - `y` — `u32`. - `w` — `u32`. - `h` — `u32`.

items.name: TextureAtlas
items.description: CPU-side bin-packing atlas for sprite regions.  # Fields - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `padding` — `u32`.  Uses a shelf-packing algorithm: regions are placed left-to-right on horizontal shelves. When a region does not fit on any existing shelf a new shelf is opened below the last one.

graphics::trail.module_doc: Trail renderer for fading ribbon effects.  Stores a series of timestamped points that age out over a configurable lifetime, producing a tapered ribbon from head to tail.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for trail-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `TrailPoint`, `Trail`. Primary functions: `new()`, `push_point()`, `update()`, `set_width()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: TrailPoint
items.description: A point in a trail with age tracking.  # Fields - `x` — `f32`. - `y` — `f32`. - `age` — `f32`.

items.name: Trail
items.description: Fading textured ribbon renderer. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `points` — `Vec<TrailPoint>`. - `lifetime` — `f32`. - `start_width` — `f32`. - `end_width` — `f32`. - `head_color` — `Color`. - `tail_color` — `Color`. - `min_distance` — `f32`.  Points are pushed at the head and automatically removed once their age exceeds the configured lifetime. Width tapers linearly from `start_width` at the head to `end_width` at the tail.

graphics::viewport.module_doc: Virtual resolution viewport with manual transform application.  Maps a fixed game resolution onto an arbitrary window size using letterboxing, stretching, or pixel-perfect scaling.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for viewport-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ScaleMode`, `Viewport`. Primary functions: `new()`, `resize()`, `get_scale()`, `get_offset()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ScaleMode
items.description: Scale mode for virtual resolution mapping.  # Variants - `Letterbox` — Letterbox variant. - `Stretch` — Stretch variant. - `PixelPerfect` — PixelPerfect variant.

items.name: Viewport
items.description: Virtual resolution with manual transform application.  # Fields - `game_width` — `f32`. - `game_height` — `f32`. - `scale_mode` — `ScaleMode`. - `scale_x` — `f32`. - `scale_y` — `f32`. - `offset_x` — `f32`. - `offset_y` — `f32`.  Maintains a mapping between a fixed game coordinate space and the actual window pixel dimensions. Call `resize()` whenever the window size changes.

graphics::viewport_scale.module_doc: Virtual resolution viewport with automatic scaling and transform stack integration.  Like `Viewport`, but also tracks the scaled content dimensions for use with an automatic graphics transform stack.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for viewport scale-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ViewportScale`. Primary functions: `new()`, `resize()`, `get_game_dimensions()`, `get_scaled_dimensions()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ViewportScale
items.description: Virtual resolution with automatic graphics stack management.  # Fields - `game_width` — `f32`. - `game_height` — `f32`. - `mode` — `ScaleMode`. - `scale_x` — `f32`. - `scale_y` — `f32`. - `offset_x` — `f32`. - `offset_y` — `f32`. - `scaled_width` — `f32`. - `scaled_height` — `f32`.  Extends the basic viewport concept by also computing `scaled_width` and `scaled_height`, which represent the game area in window pixels after scaling.

image::image_data.module_doc: CPU-side RGBA8 pixel buffer for image manipulation.  This module is part of Luna2D's `image` subsystem and provides the implementation details for image data-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ImageData`. Primary functions: `new()`, `from_file()`, `from_bytes()`, `width()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ImageData
items.description: CPU-side pixel buffer in RGBA8 format. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Stores pixel data in row-major order, 4 bytes per pixel (R, G, B, A). Can be created empty, from a file, or from raw bytes.  # Fields - `width` — `u32`. - `height` — `u32`. - `pixels` — `Vec<u8>`.

image.module_doc: CPU-side pixel-level image manipulation.  Provides `ImageData` for reading and writing individual pixels in RGBA8 format.  This module is part of Luna2D's `image` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: image_data
items.description: RGBA8 pixel buffer with per-pixel read/write access.

input::gamepad.module_doc: Gamepad implementation for the `input` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for gamepad-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `GamepadState`. Primary functions: `new()`, `update_button()`, `update_axis()`, `is_button_pressed()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: GamepadState
items.description: Holds the current button and axis state for a single gamepad identified by its id.  # Fields - `id` — Platform-assigned controller identifier. - `name` — Human-readable controller name (e.g. "Xbox Controller"). - `connected` — Whether the gamepad is currently connected. - `buttons` — *(private)* Map of button index to pressed state. - `axes` — *(private)* Map of axis index to value in `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: gilrs_button_to_string
items.description: Converts a `gilrs::Button` to a engine-compatible string name.  # Parameters - `button` — `gilrs::Button`.  # Returns `&'static str`.

items.name: gilrs_axis_to_string
items.description: Converts a `gilrs::Axis` to a engine-compatible string name.  # Parameters - `axis` — `gilrs::Axis`.  # Returns `&'static str`.

input::keyboard.module_doc: Keyboard implementation for the `input` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for keyboard-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `KeyboardState`. Primary functions: `new()`, `begin_frame()`, `press_scancode()`, `release_scancode()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: KeyboardState
items.description: Tracks which keyboard keys are currently down, just pressed, or just released.  Also tracks physical scancodes, key repeat, and text input state.  # Fields - `keys_down` — `HashSet<String>`. - `keys_pressed` — `Vec<String>`. - `keys_released` — `Vec<String>`. - `scancodes_down` — `HashSet<String>`. - `scancodes_pressed` — `Vec<String>`. - `scancodes_released` — `Vec<String>`. - `key_repeat_enabled` — `bool`. - `text_input_enabled` — `bool`. - `text_input_buffer` — `Vec<String>`.

items.name: get_scancode_from_key
items.description: Resolves a logical Luna key name to the closest physical scancode string.  # Parameters - `crate` — parameter.  # Returns `Option<String>`.

items.name: get_key_from_scancode
items.description: Resolves a physical scancode string to the closest logical Luna key name.  # Parameters - `crate` — parameter.  # Returns `Option<String>`.

items.name: winit_key_to_string
items.description: Converts a `winit 0.30` logical `Key` to the lowercase string name used by the `luna.*` API.  Returns `Some(name)` for recognised keys, `None` for keys without a mapping (which the engine skips silently).  # Parameters - `key` — A reference to the `winit::keyboard::Key` from a `WindowEvent::KeyboardInput`.  # Returns `Option<String>` — Lowercase key name (e.g. `"space"`, `"escape"`, `"a"`) or `None`.

items.name: winit_scancode_to_string
items.description: Converts a `winit 0.30` physical `KeyCode` to a engine-compatible scancode string.  Scancodes represent physical key positions and are layout-independent.  # Parameters - `code` — A `winit::keyboard::KeyCode` from the physical key field.  # Returns `Option<&'static str>` — Scancode string or `None` for unmapped keys.

input.module_doc: Mod implementation for the `input` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: gamepad
items.description: Gamepad button and axis state for a single controller.

items.name: keyboard
items.description: Keyboard state tracking pressed, held, and released keys.

items.name: mouse
items.description: Mouse position and button state management.

items.name: touch
items.description: Touch input state tracking for touchscreens.

input::mouse.module_doc: Mouse implementation for the `input` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mouse-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SystemCursor`, `MouseState`. Primary functions: `from_name()`, `as_str()`, `new()`, `begin_frame()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SystemCursor
items.description: Standard system cursor shapes. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Arrow` — Arrow variant. - `IBeam` — IBeam variant. - `Wait` — Wait variant. - `Crosshair` — Crosshair variant. - `Hand` — Hand variant. - `SizeNWSE` — SizeNWSE variant. - `SizeNESW` — SizeNESW variant. - `SizeWE` — SizeWE variant. - `SizeNS` — SizeNS variant. - `SizeAll` — SizeAll variant. - `No` — No variant.

items.name: MouseState
items.description: Tracks mouse cursor position and per-button pressed/down/released state.  # Fields - `x` — `f32`. - `y` — `f32`. - `buttons` — `[bool; 5]`. - `buttons_pressed` — `[bool; 5]`. - `buttons_released` — `[bool; 5]`. - `visible` — `bool`. - `grabbed` — `bool`. - `relative_mode` — `bool`. - `scroll_x` — `f64`. - `scroll_y` — `f64`. - `cursor_type` — `SystemCursor`.  Supports five buttons (indices 0 = left, 1 = right, 2 = middle, 3 = back/button4, 4 = forward/button5).

input::touch.module_doc: Touch input state tracking for Luna2D.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for touch-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `TouchPoint`, `TouchState`. Primary functions: `new()`, `touch_start()`, `touch_move()`, `touch_end()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: TouchPoint
items.description: Information about a single touch point. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `id` — `u64`. - `x` — `f64`. - `y` — `f64`. - `pressure` — `f64`.

items.name: TouchState
items.description: Tracks active touch points. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `touches` — `HashMap<u64`.

inventory::container.module_doc: InventoryEntry containers with configurable modes.  This module is part of Luna2D's `inventory` subsystem and provides the implementation details for container-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ContainerMode`, `Container`. Primary functions: `from_str()`, `as_str()`, `new()`, `slot_count()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ContainerMode
items.description: Container storage mode. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Fixed` — Fixed variant. - `Unlimited` — Unlimited variant. - `Starts` — Starts variant. - `Expandable` — Expandable variant.

items.name: Container
items.description: A named collection of `Slot`s. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `mode` — `ContainerMode`. - `slots` — `Vec<Slot>`. - `weight_limit` — `f64`. - `max_slots` — `u32`.

inventory::inventory.module_doc: Inventory: the top-level item storage with subsystem management.  This module is part of Luna2D's `inventory` subsystem and provides the implementation details for inventory-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SubsystemFlags`, `Inventory`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add_container()`, `get_container()`, `get_container_mut()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SubsystemFlags
items.description: Active subsystem flags for an `Inventory`.  # Fields - `weight` — `bool`. - `size` — `bool`. - `stacking` — `bool`. - `sets` — `bool`.

items.name: Inventory
items.description: Top-level inventory managing containers, equip slots, item sets, and callbacks.  # Fields - `containers` — `HashMap<String`. - `container_order` — `Vec<String>`. - `equip_slots` — `HashMap<String`. - `equip_slot_order` — `Vec<String>`. - `item_sets` — `Vec<ItemSet>`. - `subsystems` — `SubsystemFlags`.

inventory::item.module_doc: Item definition and item stack.  This module is part of Luna2D's `inventory` subsystem and provides the implementation details for item-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `InventoryEntry`, `ItemStack`. Primary functions: `new()`, `has_tag()`, `add_tag()`, `remove_tag()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: InventoryEntry
items.description: A single item definition with type, tags, weight, size, and stack limit.  # Fields - `item_type` — `String`. - `tags` — `Vec<String>`. - `weight` — `f64`. - `size_w` — `u32`. - `size_h` — `u32`. - `stack_limit` — `u32`.

items.name: ItemStack
items.description: A counted stack of a single Item type.  # Fields - `item` — `InventoryEntry`. - `quantity` — `u32`. - `max_quantity` — `u32`.

inventory::item_set.module_doc: Item sets, set requirements, and subsystem flags.  This module is part of Luna2D's `inventory` subsystem and provides the implementation details for item set-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SetRequirement`, `ItemSet`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add_requirement()`, `is_active()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SetRequirement
items.description: A single requirement in an `ItemSet`. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `tag` — `String`. - `slot_filter` — `String`.

items.name: ItemSet
items.description: A named set defining bonus conditions: all requirements must be satisfied simultaneously.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `requirements` — `Vec<SetRequirement>`.

inventory.module_doc: Inventory system — slot-based item containers, equipment slots, item sets, subsystem toggles (weight, size, stacking, sets), and callback hooks.  This module is part of Luna2D's `inventory` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: item
items.description: Item definition and item stacks.

items.name: slot
items.description: Inventory slot and slot state.

items.name: container
items.description: Item containers.

items.name: item_set
items.description: Item sets, set requirements, subsystem flags.

items.name: inventory
items.description: Top-level inventory with subsystem management.

inventory::slot.module_doc: Inventory slots and slot state.  This module is part of Luna2D's `inventory` subsystem and provides the implementation details for slot-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SlotState`, `Slot`. Primary functions: `from_str()`, `as_str()`, `new()`, `is_empty()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SlotState
items.description: Valid state strings for a slot. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Slot` — Slot variant. - `Active` — Active variant. - `Passive` — Passive variant. - `Idle` — Idle variant.

items.name: Slot
items.description: A single inventory position that holds an optional `ItemStack`.  # Fields - `slot_type` — `String`. - `state` — `SlotState`. - `capacity_w` — `u32`. - `capacity_h` — `u32`. - `stack` — `Option<ItemStack>`.

item::builder.module_doc: Stack builder — template-based stack construction with validation.  A `StackBuilder` accumulates `BuildEntry` records (item type + count + optional overrides) and either builds a ready-to-use `Stack` or validates an existing stack against construction constraints.

items.name: BuildEntry
items.description: One "slot" in a stack template: an item type plus a count and optional overrides.  # Fields - `type_name` — `String`. - `count` — `usize`. - `stat_overrides` — `HashMap<String`. - `extra_tags` — `Vec<String>`. - `extra_metadata` — `HashMap<String`.

items.name: StackBuilder
items.description: Builds a `Stack` from `BuildEntry` templates and validates construction constraints.  Constraints (min/max size, max copies per type, banned/required types) are all optional and default to "unconstrained".  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `entries` — `Vec<BuildEntry>`. - `shuffle_on_build` — `bool`. - `min_size` — `usize`. - `max_size` — `usize`. - `max_copies` — `usize`. - `per_type_limits` — `HashMap<String`. - `required_types` — `Vec<String>`. - `banned_types` — `Vec<String>`. - `banned_categories` — `Vec<String>`. - `max_per_category` — `HashMap<String`.

item::group.module_doc: Group analysis — stat-based and tag-based grouping of item slices.  All functions operate on plain `&[Item]` slices and return index-based groups so that the caller can decide what to do with them without taking ownership.  These utilities are domain-agnostic: "n-of-a-stat" works for poker hands, board game scoring, loot classification, or any other scenario.

items.name: ItemGroup
items.description: A labelled subset of an item slice, referenced by index.  Indices refer to positions in the original `&[Item]` slice passed to the analysis function.  # Fields - `label` — `String`. - `indices` — `Vec<usize>`. - `score` — `u32`.

items.name: group_by_stat
items.description: Group item indices by the integer part of a named stat.  Returns a map from `stat_value as i64` to a list of 0-based indices.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`. - `stat` — `&str`.  # Returns `HashMap<i64, Vec<usize>>`.

items.name: group_by_category
items.description: Group item indices by category. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Returns a map from category string to a list of 0-based indices.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`.  # Returns `HashMap<String, Vec<usize>>`.

items.name: group_by_tag_prefix
items.description: Group item indices by a tag prefix (the part before the first `:`).  Tags matching `prefix:value` are grouped under `prefix`.  Tags without the prefix separator are grouped under the tag itself.  Example: items tagged `"suit:hearts"`, `"suit:spades"` both group under `"suit"`.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`. - `prefix` — `&str`.  # Returns `HashMap<String, Vec<usize>>`.

items.name: find_n_of_stat
items.description: Find all maximal groups where items share the exact same integer stat value, and the group has exactly `n` members.  Analogous to "n-of-a-kind" in card games.  Useful for any matching scenario.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`. - `stat` — `&str`. - `n` — `usize`.  # Returns `Vec<ItemGroup>`.

items.name: find_at_least_n_of_stat
items.description: Find all groups where at least `n` items share the same integer stat value.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`. - `stat` — `&str`. - `n` — `usize`.  # Returns `Vec<ItemGroup>`.

items.name: find_sequences
items.description: Find all runs (sequences of consecutive integer stat values) of length ≥ `min_run`.  Sorts items by stat value and identifies consecutive runs. Returns one `ItemGroup` per run.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`. - `stat` — `&str`. - `in_run` — `usize`.  # Returns `Vec<ItemGroup>`.

items.name: find_tag_groups
items.description: Find groups of items that all share the same tag-prefix value and contain at least `min_size` members.  Analogous to "flush" detection: all items with the same `suit:hearts` tag form one group.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`. - `ag_prefix` — `&str`. - `in_size` — `usize`.  # Returns `Vec<ItemGroup>`.

items.name: sorted_indices_by_stat
items.description: Return a sorted list of 0-based indices; does not modify the slice.  `ascending = true` → lowest stat first.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`. - `stat` — `&str`. - `ascending` — `bool`.  # Returns `Vec<usize>`.

items.name: sorted_indices_by_category
items.description: Return sorted indices grouped alphabetically by category.  # Parameters - `items` — `&[Item]`.  # Returns `Vec<usize>`.

item::history.module_doc: Change history — append-only log of stack mutations.  `StackHistory` is kept separate from `Stack` so that the caller decides what (if anything) to track.  Wrap it in an `Rc<RefCell<StackHistory>>` and pass it around alongside stacks.

items.name: HistoryAction
items.description: The category of change that was recorded.  # Variants - `An` — An variant. - `Pushed` — Pushed variant. - `Popped` — Popped variant. - `Moved` — Moved variant. - `A` — A variant. - `Shuffled` — Shuffled variant. - `Sorted` — Sorted variant. - `Cleared` — Cleared variant. - `Built` — Built variant. - `User` — User variant. - `Custom` — Custom variant.

items.name: HistoryEntry
items.description: A single entry in the history log. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `seq` — `u64`. - `stack_name` — `String`. - `action` — `HistoryAction`. - `size_after` — `usize`.

items.name: StackHistory
items.description: Append-only change log with an optional rolling size limit.  # Fields - `entries` — `VecDeque<HistoryEntry>`. - `max_size` — `Option<usize>`. - `next_seq` — `u64`.

item::item.module_doc: Generic item type definitions, the global type registry, and item instances.  An `Item` is a named bundle of stats, tags, counters, and metadata. The engine never interprets these fields — all semantic meaning is user-defined in Lua.  An item might represent a card, tile, token, ingredient, power-up, or any other game object.

items.name: ItemTypeDef
items.description: Template that describes a class of items (the "blueprint").  Stats, tags, and metadata defined here are seeded into every new `Item` created with `Item::new(type_name)`.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `category` — `String`. - `base_stats` — `HashMap<String`. - `base_tags` — `Vec<String>`. - `metadata` — `HashMap<String`.

items.name: define_item_type
items.description: Register (or overwrite) an item type in the thread-local type registry.  # Parameters - `name` — `impl Into<String>`. - `def` — `ItemTypeDef`.

items.name: get_item_type
items.description: Look up an item type definition by name. Returns `None` if not found.  # Parameters - `name` — `&str`.  # Returns `Option<ItemTypeDef>`.

items.name: get_item_type_names
items.description: Return all registered type names. This accessor incurs no allocation; call it freely in hot paths.  # Returns `Vec<String>`.

items.name: clear_item_types
items.description: Remove all entries from the registry. After this call the container is in the same state as immediately after construction.

items.name: Item
items.description: A single item instance. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  All stat names, tag names, counter names, and metadata keys are user-defined.  The engine never attaches meaning to them.  # Fields - `item_type` — `String`. - `name` — `String`. - `category` — `String`. - `stats` — `HashMap<String`. - `tags` — `Vec<String>`. - `counters` — `HashMap<String`. - `metadata` — `HashMap<String`. - `owner` — `String`. - `slot` — `String`.

item::manager.module_doc: Stack manager — organises multiple named `Stack` instances as a unit.  Useful for modelling a board where several stacks (draw pile, hand, discard, play area, etc.) coexist and items need to move between them.

items.name: StackManager
items.description: Manages a collection of named `Stack` instances.  All stack names and their semantics are user-defined.  # Fields - `stacks` — `HashMap<String`.

item.module_doc: Generic item-centric data structures.  Provides the building blocks for any system that manages a set of named, tagged, stat-carrying objects (items) organised into stacks/piles.  # Key types  | Type | Purpose | |---|---| | [`Item`] | A named game object with stats, tags, counters, metadata | | [`ItemTypeDef`] | Blueprint/template registered in the global type registry | | [`Stack`] | Ordered collection of items (draw pile, hand, queue, …) | | [`StackBuilder`] | Template-based stack construction with validation | | [`StackManager`] | Collection of named stacks; coordinates movement | | [`ItemPool`] | Weighted random pool of item types | | [`Slot`] | Bounded single-position holder for items | | [`StackHistory`] | Append-only change log | | [`ItemGroup`] | Index-list for grouping analysis results |  # No gameplay assumptions  This module deliberately contains no player, turn, trick, rule, or scoring semantics.  All such logic belongs in Lua scripts that use these structures.

items.name: item
items.description: Item sub-module.

items.name: stack
items.description: Stack sub-module.

items.name: builder
items.description: Builder sub-module.

items.name: manager

item::pool.module_doc: Weighted item-type pool for random selection.  An `ItemPool` holds a set of item type names each with a relative draw weight.  Useful for loot tables, draft formats, booster generation, or any weighted-random-choice scenario.

items.name: PoolEntry
items.description: A single entry in an item pool. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `type_name` — `String`. - `weight` — `u32`.

items.name: ItemPool
items.description: A pool of item types for weighted random draws.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `entries` — `Vec<PoolEntry>`.

item::slot.module_doc: Slot — a bounded named position that holds a small set of items.  A slot is like a named cell on a board: it has an optional capacity limit and enforces it on push.  Useful for equipment slots, board positions, hand slots, or any place where "there can only be N items here" matters.

items.name: Slot
items.description: A bounded named position holding zero or more items.  Semantics (what "this slot" means) are entirely user-defined.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `capacity` — `Option<usize>`. - `items` — `Vec<Item>`.

item::stack.module_doc: Ordered item collection — a stack, pile, hand, queue, or any linear sequence of items.  The last element is the "top" and the first element is the "bottom". All semantics (draw pile, discard pile, hand, queue) are user-defined.

items.name: Stack
items.description: An ordered collection of items. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  `items[last]` = top, `items[0]` = bottom.  # Fields - `name` — `String`. - `items` — `Vec<Item>`. - `capacity` — `Option<usize>`.

root.module_doc: Luna2D engine binary entry point.  This crate is a thin wrapper around the `luna2d` library crate. Its only job is to call [`luna2d::luna_run`], which installs the panic hook, parses the command-line game-directory argument, loads `conf.lua`, and enters the main engine loop.  Two binary crates share the same source via `Cargo.toml` feature flags:   - `luna`   — console-attached binary (default, used during development)   - `lunec`  — no-console binary (Windows release build, no terminal window)  To run a game pass the path to the game directory as the first CLI argument:  ```text luna path/to/my_game ```  If no argument is given, the engine looks for `main.lua` in the current working directory and falls back to the splash screen if none is found.

Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for AI subsystems: AIWorld, Agent, Blackboard, StateMachine, BehaviorTree, SteeringManager, PathGrid, FlowField, QLearner, UtilityAI, GOAPPlanner, InfluenceMap, Squad, and CommandQueue.

This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation details for audio api-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `LuaSource`, `LuaBus`, `LuaMidiPlayer`. Primary functions: `register()`.  All public items are documented. items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for an audio source resource.  # Fields - `state` — `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`. - `key` — `SoundKey`.  Wraps a `SoundKey` and shared state reference so the Lua side can call methods like `source:play()`, `source:setVolume()` directly.

Wraps a `BusKey` and shared state reference so the Lua side can call methods like `bus:setVolume()`, `bus:isPaused()` directly.

items.description: Registers all `luna.audio.*` functions into the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`. - `state` — `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.

Key types exported from this module: `LuaImage`, `LuaNineSlice`, `LuaFont`, `LuaSpriteBatch`, `LuaCanvas`. Primary functions: `texture_key_from_value()`, `font_key_from_value()`, `batch_key_from_value()`, `canvas_key_from_value()`.  All public items are documented. Stores the source texture key, border insets, and texture dimensions so the engine can draw the image stretched to any size while preserving corner and edge proportions.  # Fields - `state` — `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`. - `texture_key` — `TextureKey`. - `top` — `f32`. - `right` — `f32`. - `bottom` — `f32`. - `left` — `f32`. - `tex_width` — `f32`. - `tex_height` — `f32`.

items.name: LuaCanvas
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for an off-screen canvas resource.  # Fields - `state` ÔÇö `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`. - `key` ÔÇö `CanvasKey`.

lua_api.module_doc: Mod implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `FullscreenType`, `WindowState`, `ErrorInfo`, `SharedState`. Primary functions: `new()`, `create_lua_vm()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: FullscreenType
items.description: Fullscreen mode type for window management.  # Variants - `Desktop` — Desktop variant. - `Exclusive` — Exclusive variant.

items.name: WindowState
items.description: Tracks window state and queues window operations for the event loop.  # Fields - `focused` — `bool`. - `mouse_focused` — `bool`. - `minimized` — `bool`. - `maximized` — `bool`. - `visible` — `bool`. - `dpi_scale` — `f64`. - `position_x` — `i32`. - `position_y` — `i32`. - `pending_title` — `Option<String>`. - `pending_fullscreen` — `Option<bool>`. - `pending_fullscreen_type` — `FullscreenType`. - `pending_position` — `Option<(i32, i32)>`. - `pending_size` — `Option<(u32, u32)>`. - `pending_minimize` — `bool`. - `pending_maximize` — `bool`. - `pending_restore` — `bool`. - `pending_close` — `bool`. - `pending_attention` — `bool`. - `pending_icon_path` — `Option<String>`. - `vsync_mode` — `i32`. - `pending_vsync` — `Option<i32>`. - `fullscreen` — `bool`. - `fullscreen_type` — `FullscreenType`.  Query fields are written by `app.rs` from window events and read by Lua. Pending fields are written by Lua closures and consumed by `app.rs`.

items.name: ErrorInfo
items.description: Structured error information for the last engine error.  # Fields - `message` — `String`. - `code` — `String`. - `category` — `String`. - `hint` — `Option<String>`.

items.name: SharedState
items.description: Shared mutable state passed via `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>` to all Lua API closures and the engine loop.  # Fields - `draw_commands` — Queue of pending `DrawCommand` values, flushed each frame. - `current_color` — Active RGBA draw color `[r, g, b, a]`. - `background_color` — Screen clear color set by `luna.graphics.setBackgroundColor`. - `textures` — Loaded texture pixel data, indexed by `Texture::id`. - `keys_down` — Set of currently held key name strings. - `mouse` — Mouse cursor position, button state, scroll, and cursor settings. - `delta_time` — Elapsed time for the current frame in seconds. - `total_time` — Accumulated time since engine start in seconds. - `fps` — Rolling frames-per-second measurement. - `window_width` — Current window width in pixels. - `window_height` — Current window height in pixels. - `window_title` — Current window title string. - `mixer` — The rodio audio mixer managing all sound sources. - `game_dir` — Absolute path to the game directory. - `quit_requested` — Set to `true` by `luna.event.quit()` to end the game loop. - `exit_code` — Exit code to return when `quit_requested` is `true`. - `line_width` — Current stroke width for outline draw commands. - `blend_mode` — Current blend mode for draw operations. - `fonts` — Loaded TTF fonts for text rendering. - `active_font` — Index of the currently active font (`None` = use bitmap fallback). - `canvases` — Off-screen render targets (canvases) for compositing. - `gamepads` — Connected gamepad state instances.  Shared mutable state accessible by both the engine loop and Lua closures.

items.name: create_lua_vm
items.description: Creates and configures the Lua VM, registers all `luna.*` sub-APIs, and returns the ready `Lua` instance.  # Parameters - `state` — Shared engine state passed (via `Rc<RefCell>` clone) to every Lua closure.  # Returns `LuaResult<Lua>` — A configured Lua VM with `luna.*` as a global, or a Lua error if any sub-API fails to register.

lua_api::particle_api::helpers.module_doc: Helper functions for the particle API.  Private utilities shared between `mod.rs` (register first half) and `ext.rs` (register second half).  This module is part of Luna2D's `particle_api` subsystem and provides the implementation details for helpers-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `LuaParticleSystem`. Primary functions: `invalid_particle_handle()`, `ensure_particle_exists()`, `require_particle_key()`, `particle_system()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: invalid_particle_handle
items.description: Returns a `LuaError` for an invalid particle handle.  # Returns `LuaError`.  # Parameters - `function_name` — `&str`

items.name: ensure_particle_exists
items.description: Return `LuaError` if the particle does not exist in the pool.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.  # Parameters - `particle_systems` — `&SlotMap<ParticleKey, ParticleSystem>` - `key` — `ParticleKey` - `function_name` — `&str`

items.name: require_particle_key
items.description: Resolve and validate a particle key, returning `LuaError` if missing.  # Returns `LuaResult<ParticleKey>`.  # Parameters - `particle_systems` — `&SlotMap<ParticleKey, ParticleSystem>` - `val` — `&LuaValue` - `function_name` — `&str`

items.name: particle_system
items.description: Borrow the particle system from shared state.  # Returns `LuaResult<&'a ParticleSystem>`.  # Parameters - `particle_systems` — `&'a SlotMap<ParticleKey, ParticleSystem>` - `key` — `ParticleKey` - `function_name` — `&str`

items.name: particle_system_mut
items.description: Borrow the particle system (mutable) from shared state.  # Returns `LuaResult<&'a mut ParticleSystem>`.  # Parameters - `particle_systems` — `&'a mut SlotMap<ParticleKey, ParticleSystem>` - `key` — `ParticleKey` - `function_name` — `&str`

items.name: particle_key_from_value
items.description: Extract a `ParticleKey` from either a `LuaParticleSystem` UserData or a numeric ID.  Callers validate liveness against their current `SharedState` borrow so stale-handle errors keep the right function context and never re-borrow the state from userdata.  # Returns `LuaResult<ParticleKey>`.  # Parameters - `val` — `&LuaValue`

items.name: parse_color
items.description: Helper: parse a Lua color table `{r, g, b, a}` into `[f32; 4]`.  # Parameters - `c` — `&LuaTable` with RGBA components at indices 1–4. - `default_a` — `f32` fallback alpha when index 4 is absent.  # Returns `LuaResult<[f32; 4]>`.

items.name: LuaParticleSystem
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a particle system resource.  # Fields - `state` ÔÇö `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`. - `key` ÔÇö `ParticleKey`.  Wraps a `ParticleKey` and shared state reference so the Lua side can call methods like `ps:update(dt)`, `ps:start()` directly.

items.name: parse_emission_shape
items.description: Parse an emission shape from a Lua string name and optional parameters table.  # Returns `EmissionShape`.  # Parameters - `shape` — `&str` - `args` — `Option<&LuaTable>`

items.name: emission_shape_to_lua
items.description: Convert an `EmissionShape` to a Lua table with type and parameter fields.  # Returns `LuaResult<LuaTable<'lua>>`.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&'lua Lua` - `shape` — `&EmissionShape`

items.name: lua_value_to_f64
items.description: Helper to extract an f64 from a `LuaValue`.  # Returns `Option<f64>`.  # Parameters - `v` — `&LuaValue`

items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a quest. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.

items.name: LuaQuestLog
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a quest log. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.

items.name: register
items.description: Register `luna.quest.*` API with the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.

lua_api::resource_api.module_doc: Lua API bindings for the `luna.resource.*` resource economy module.  Provides `ResourceManager` UserData for managing named resources with capacity, flow rates, decay, interest, conversions, and overflow policies.

items.name: LuaResourceManager
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a resource economy manager.  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<ResourceManager>>`.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers the `luna.resource` module with the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.

lua_api::savegame_api.module_doc: Savegame Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation details for savegame api-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `register()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers `luna.savegame.*` functions into the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`. - `_state` — `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.  Provides a slot-based save/load system with collectors, schema versioning, dirty tracking, and auto-save.

lua_api::scene_api.module_doc: Registers the `luna.scene.*` scene stack, registry, data store, and depth-sorter API.  The scene stack manages a LIFO stack of Lua scene tables with optional visual transitions. Scene tables are stored via `mlua::RegistryKey` and identified by `SceneId`. Inter-scene data is stored similarly.  The `DepthSorter` UserData batches draw callbacks by depth for ordered compositing each frame.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers the `luna.scene` table with scene stack, registry, data store,  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`. and depth-sorter factory functions.

lua_api::sound_api.module_doc: Registers the `luna.sound.*` decoded audio sample manipulation API.  This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation details for sound api-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `register()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers the `luna.sound` table on the provided `luna` namespace.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`. - `state` — `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.

lua_api::stats_api.module_doc: Lua API bindings for the `luna.stats` RPG character sheet system.  Exposes [`Sheet`] and a global [`StatsRegistry`] as `luna.stats.*` Lua values.  This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation details for stats api-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `LuaSheet`. Primary functions: `register()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: LuaSheet
items.description: Lua-visible wrapper for a character [`Sheet`].  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<Sheet>>`. - `registry` — `Rc<RefCell<StatsRegistry>>`.

items.name: register
items.description: Register the `luna.stats` module into the Lua state.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.

lua_api::system_api.module_doc: System Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation details for system api-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `PowerState`. Primary functions: `get_processor_count()`, `get_memory_size()`, `open_url()`, `get_preferred_locales()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: get_processor_count
items.description: Returns the number of logical processors available.  # Returns Logical CPU count; at least 1.

items.name: get_memory_size
items.description: Returns total system RAM in MiB using the `sysinfo` crate.  # Returns Physical memory in MiB (e.g. 16384 for 16 GB).

items.name: open_url
items.description: Opens a URL in the default browser/application.  Only `http://`, `https://`, and `mailto:` schemes are allowed.  # Parameters - `url` — the URL string to open  # Returns `true` if the command was spawned successfully; `false` if the scheme is rejected or the spawn failed.

items.name: get_preferred_locales
items.description: Returns the user's preferred locale strings.  # Returns A `Vec<String>` with at least one locale tag (e.g. `"en_US"`).

items.name: PowerState
items.description: Power state of the device. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Unknown` — cannot determine power state - `Battery` — running on battery - `NoBattery` — no battery (desktop plugged in) - `Charging` — battery is charging - `Charged` — battery is fully charged

items.name: get_power_info
items.description: Returns power/battery information: (state, percent, seconds).  On desktop platforms this returns `(Unknown, None, None)`.  # Returns A tuple of `(PowerState, Option<u32>, Option<u32>)` — the power state, remaining battery percentage (0–100), and remaining seconds on battery.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers `luna.system.*` platform query functions into the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — The active Lua VM instance. - `luna` — The `luna` global table to attach functions to.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>` — Ok if all functions were registered successfully; Lua error otherwise.

lua_api::thread_api.module_doc: Registers the `luna.thread` namespace.  Provides Lua-level multithreading: create background threads that run independent Lua VMs and communicate via thread-safe channels.  This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation details for thread api-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `LuaThreadHandle`. Primary functions: `register()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: LuaThreadHandle
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a background thread handle.  # Fields - `inner` — `Arc<Mutex<LuaThread>>`.  Wraps `Arc<Mutex<LuaThread>>` so multiple Lua references can share access to the same thread state.

items.name: register
items.description: Registers all `luna.thread.*` functions into the Lua VM.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.  # Functions registered - `luna.thread.newThread(code)` — create a new background thread - `luna.thread.newChannel()` — create an unnamed channel - `luna.thread.getChannel(name)` — get or create a named global channel

lua_api::tilemap_api::ext.module_doc: Extended tilemap API registrations (second half of `register`).

items.name: register_ext
items.description: Register extended Lua bindings for this sub-module.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua` - `tilemap_table` — `&LuaTable`

lua_api::tilemap_api::helpers.module_doc: Helper types and utilities for the tilemap API.  Contains `LuaUserData` implementations for tilemap wrapper types.

items.name: LuaTileSet
items.description: Lua wrapper around a [`TileSet`]. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<TileSet>>`.

items.name: LuaTileMap
items.description: Lua wrapper around a [`TileMap`]. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `inner` ÔÇö `Rc<RefCell<TileMap>>`.

items.name: LuaAutoTileSheet
items.description: Lua wrapper around an [`AutoTileSheet`].  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<AutoTileSheet>>`.

items.name: LuaMapBlock
items.description: Lua wrapper around a [`MapBlock`]. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<MapBlock>>`.

items.name: LuaMapGroup
items.description: Lua wrapper around a [`MapGroup`]. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<MapGroup>>`.

items.name: LuaMapScript
items.description: Lua wrapper around a [`MapScript`]. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<MapScript>>`.

items.name: LuaMapGen
items.description: Lua wrapper around a [`MapGen`], storing the associated group for generation.  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<MapGen>>`. - `group` — `Rc<RefCell<MapGroup>>`.

items.name: LuaChunkMap
items.description: Lua wrapper around a [`ChunkMap`]. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<ChunkMap>>`.

items.name: LuaIsoMap
items.description: Lua wrapper around an [`IsoMap`]. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `inner` — `Rc<RefCell<IsoMap>>`.

items.name: rect_to_table
items.description: Convert a `Rect` into a Lua table `{x, y, w, h}`.  # Returns `LuaResult<LuaTable<'_>>`.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua` - `r` — `Rect`

items.name: parse_edge
items.description: Parse an edge tag string into a `TileEdge` enum variant.  # Returns `LuaResult<Edge>`.  # Parameters - `s` — `&str`

items.name: parse_script_step
items.description: Parses a Lua table into a [`ScriptStep`].  # Returns `LuaResult<ScriptStep>`.  # Parameters - `t` — `&LuaTable`

items.name: step_to_table
items.description: Converts a [`ScriptStep`] back to a Lua table.  # Returns `LuaResult<LuaTable<'lua>>`.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&'lua Lua` - `step` — `&ScriptStep`

items.name: ext
items.description: Ext sub-module.

items.name: register
items.description: Register `luna..*` bindings in the Lua state.  # Parameters - `lua` — `&Lua`. - `luna` — `&LuaTable`.  # Returns `LuaResult<()>`.
